name: Profile_R5_TestReport_R4
title: Cross-version Profile for R5.TestReport resource for use in FHIR R4
description: This cross-version profile allows R5 TestReport resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 TestReport resources.

element.short: Cross-version Profile for R5.TestReport resource for use in FHIR R4
element.definition: This cross-version profile allows R5 TestReport resource content to be represented via FHIR R4 TestReport resources.

element.short: R5: Canonical URL to the  version-specific TestScript that was executed to produce this TestReport additional types
element.definition: R5: `TestReport.testScript` additional types (canonical(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/TestScript)) additional types from child elements (value)
element.comment:
Element `TestReport.testScript` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `TestReport.testScript` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `TestReport.testScript` do not cover the following types: canonical.
The mappings for `TestReport.testScript` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: value.

element.short: R5: Links or references to the testing requirements (new)
element.comment:
Element `TestReport.setup.action.assert.requirement` has a context of TestReport.setup.action.assert based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `TestReport`.
Element `TestReport.setup.action.assert.requirement` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
TestScript and TestReport instances are typically (and expected to be) based on known, defined test requirements and documentation. These links provide traceability from the executable/executed TestScript and TestReport tests to these requirements.
